relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The Eternal continued answering Job.
Can the one who argues with Shaddai correct him?
Job answered.
He saw his smallness and placed his hand over his mouth.
He would add no more.
The Eternal answered again from the whirlwind.
Job is called again to stand and answer.
The question strikes the center: will Job annul divine justice to prove himself right?
Does Job have divine arm or thunder voice?
Can he clothe himself in majesty and glory?
Can he pour out anger and humble pride?
Can he bring every proud and wicked power low?
Can he bind them in dust and hiddenness?
Only then could his own right hand save him.
Behemoth appears, made by the Divine alongside Job.
Its strength lives in body and belly.
Its tail bends like cedar and its thighs are woven with power.
Its bones and limbs are bronze and iron imagery.
It is first among divine wild ways; only its Maker can approach it fully.
Mountains feed it, and field creatures play around it.
It rests hidden among reeds and marsh.
Shade and stream surround it.
Even rushing river does not panic it.
It is not easily captured or domesticated.
